channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Maguire given reduced 15-month suspended sentence for Greece incident
item.description: Manchester United defender Harry Maguire has his suspended sentence reduced to 15 months at a retrial following an incident in Greece in 2020.

item.title: Fletcher banned for six games for homophobic slur
item.description: Manchester United midfielder Jack Fletcher apologises for using a homophobic slur that has led to him receiving a six-match ban.

item.title: 'It's better to be chased' - the mentality of a title race
item.description: The Premier League title race is set to go to the wire. BBC Sport has spoken to former winners to find out how players deal with the pressure.

item.title: Liverpool top-five failure 'could cost £120m'
item.description: Failure to qualify for the Champions League would have financial implications for Liverpool. How much would it impact them?

item.title: Man Utd eye wingers, after getting rid under Amorim
item.description: Manchester United are likely to look at signing a left winger this summer even though former head coach Ruben Amorim spent three windows getting rid of them.

item.title: Galatasaray fans banned from Liverpool second leg
item.description: Galatasaray fans are banned from the club's Champions League visit to Liverpool after disturbances during their game with Juventus in the previous round.

item.title: Calls for lifetime ban on coach who secretly filmed women players
item.description: Victims of a coach who avoided prison despite secretly filming women footballers in changing rooms and showers are calling for him to receive a global lifetime ban.

item.title: Why does Scotland lead the way on heading in football?
item.description: BBC Scotland sports news correspondent Chris McLaughlin examines the landscape about the impact of heading the ball in Scottish football.

item.title: Tranmere sack manager Crosby after losing run
item.description: Tranmere Rovers sack manager Andy Crosby, 10 months after he was appointed on a permanent basis.

item.title: 'I had the heart pumping' - Warnock returns to management at 77
item.description: Neil Warnock reflects on his first game back as a manager as the 77-year-old guides Torquay United to a 2-2 draw against Farnborough.

item.title: Does 'class' captain Weir hold the key to Scotland's World Cup return?
item.description: Caroline Weir again showed how vital she is to Scotland as the new permanent captain scored a hat-trick in their 5-0 win over Luxembourg at the start of their World Cup qualification campaign.

item.title: Wolves score late winner to secure shock win against Liverpool
item.description: Andre scores a 94th minute winner as bottom club Wolves beat Liverpool 2-1 at Molineux to secure just their third win in the Premier League this season.

item.title: Everton beat Burnley to secure first home win of 2026
item.description: Everton put two past bottom-of-the-table Burnley to win 2-0 and secure their first home victory of 2026.

item.title: Diarra penalty enough to give Sunderland victory at Leeds
item.description: Habib Diarra scores the only goal from the penalty spot to give Sunderland a 1-0 win against Leeds at Elland Road in the Premier League.

item.title: Bournemouth held to frustrating goalless draw by Brentford
item.description: Bournemouth hit the post twice and have a goal disallowed as they draw 0-0 against Brentford at the Vitality Stadium in the Premier League.

item.title: Barcelona step up Van de Ven interest - Wednesday's gossip
item.description: Barcelona to step up efforts for Tottenham defender Micky van de Ven, Bruno Fernandes future at Manchester United depends on Champions League qualification, Aston Villa keen on Fulham's Harry Wilson, plus more.

item.title: Morrison to stay West Brom boss until end of season
item.description: Championship strugglers West Bromwich Albion confirm interim head coach James Morrison will stay in charge until the end of the season.

item.title: 'The hunger is big' - Sunderland reach 40 points and want more
item.description: By reaching 40 points with victory at Leeds on Tuesday, Sunderland have pretty much ensured Premier League survival by early March.

item.title: Trump 'does not care' if Iran play at World Cup
item.description: US President Donald Trump says he does not care if Iran take part in this summer's 2026 World Cup.

item.title: WSL 2 side to host promotion play-off in May
item.description: The Women's Super League promotion play-off will be held at the home of the second-tier side on Saturday, 23 May at 12:30 BST, confirms WSL Football.

item.title: 'Same old story' for Slot as Wolves 'keep fighting'
item.description: Liverpool head coach Arne Slot began the week by saying he found most Premier League games no longer a joy to watch - and had little to enjoy as he described defeat at Wolves as the "same old story".

item.title: Weir hits hat-trick as Scotland hammer Luxembourg
item.description: Captain Caroline Weir hits a hat-trick in a comfortable win for Scotland against a limited Luxembourg side in World Cup qualifying.

item.title: Wolves winner 'not even a chance' - Slot
item.description: Liverpool manager Arne Slot says the late 2-1 defeat by Wolves followed a pattern he has seen "many times this season" with his team dominating possession and conceding from "not even a chance".

item.title: 'We'd forgotten how to win' - Edwards on 'enjoyable' victory
item.description: Wolves manager Rob Edwards praises his sides commitment to improvement, but insists they're not thinking about staying in the Premier League, despite beating Liverpool 2-1 at Molineux to give them back-to-back league wins for the first time this season.

item.title: Port Vale enjoy cup upset as they knock out Bristol City
item.description: League One strugglers Port Vale cause a cup upset as they beat Championship side Bristol City 1-0 in extra-time at Vale Park to book their place in the FA Cup fifth round for the first time in 30 years.

item.title: Moyes wants players to believe Europe is within reach
item.description: Everton manager David Moyes is pleased his side ended their winless home run with a 2-0 victory over Burnley, and says he wants his players to be looking up the table and putting some of the top teams under pressure.

item.title: England deliver Wiegman's 'clear win' to make World Cup statement
item.description: A "clear win" was what Sarina Wiegman wanted from her England side against Ukraine - and that was what they delivered in a bright start to their World Cup qualifying campaign.

item.title: The one that got away - Carrick returns to Tyneside with Man Utd
item.description: Michael Carrick will very much be a Manchester United man when he takes his side to St James' Park on Wednesday night - but the boyhood Newcastle United supporter has not forgotten his roots.

item.title: Leeds director warns regeneration is now or 'never'
item.description: Leeds director Peter Lowy urges government to back plans to regenerate the Elland Road area.

item.title: Wales aiming for World Cup qualifying winning run - Hughes
item.description: Elise Hughes came off the bench to head in a late equaliser as Wales drew 2-2 in their opening 2027 World Cup qualifier against Czech Republic.

item.title: Farke 'frustrated' as 'dominant' Leeds lose to Sunderland
item.description: Leeds United manager Daniel Farke says it felt like it was "written in the stars" that his side would lose against Sunderland, after Leeds were beaten 1-0 at Elland Road in the Premier League.

item.title: 'We fell short' - Parker frustrated as Burnley lose at Everton
item.description: Burnley manager Scott Parker admits he's "searching for answers" after watching his side lose 2-0 at Everton in the Premier League.

item.title: 'I think it's a penalty, I cannot accept it' - Iraola
item.description: Bournemouth manager Andoni Iraola says it is "difficult" to explain how Bournemouth didn't score and had to settle for a 0-0 draw with Brentford.

item.title: Andrews admits Brentford had some 'luck' after draw at Bournemouth
item.description: Brentford manager Keith Andrews praised his team's resilience, but admitted they had a bit of fortune on their side after they held Bournemouth to a 0-0 draw at the Vitality Stadium.

item.title: Arsenal set-pieces: Too effective or too boring?
item.description: On the Monday Night Club, Nedum Onuoha breaks down why some people might find Arsenal's set-pieces boring too watch.

item.title: Haaland? Kane? R9? Ranking the best centre-forwards this century
item.description: Who are the best centre-forwards of the 21st Century? We've had a go at trying to whittle down a pretty formidable list.

item.title: Republic of Ireland loss to France 'stings' - McCabe
item.description: Republic of Ireland captain Katie McCabe says the side "can't feel sorry for ourselves" after their 2-1 defeat by France at Tallaght Stadium.

item.title: How to follow the FA Cup fifth round on the BBC
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explain how you can watch, listen and follow the FA Cup fifth round.

item.title: In defence of Arsenal - is criticism of Premier League leaders unfair?
item.description: In defence of Arsenal - BBC Sport chief football writer Phil McNulty asks whether criticism of the Premier League leaders is unfair as they chase four trophies.

item.title: Satriano goal stuns Real Madrid
item.description: Real Madrid miss the chance to move within one point of La Liga leaders Barcelona after Martin Satriano's spectacular strike givse Getafe a shock win at the Bernabeu.

item.title: England given 'reassurances' about Turkey security - Wiegman
item.description: Manager Sarina Wiegman says England have been given "reassurances" that there are no safety concerns about their Women's World Cup qualifier against Ukraine being held in Turkey, as the conflict in the Middle East widens.

item.title: I don't like 'home team referee' Bramall - Tudor
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur manager Igor Tudor call into doubt the decision-making of referee Thomas Bramall, after the official allowed Fulham's opening goal to stand, and demands greater consistency on fouls given for pushing.

item.title: Hart breaks down why 'disciplined' Raya is so good
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Joe Hart breaks down how David Raya has cemented himself as one of the best goalkeepers in the Premier League.

item.title: How Fernandes 'coached' Man Utd to victory over Palace
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Joe Hart analyses how Bruno Fernandes was a "coach on the pitch" to "get the best" out of his Manchester United team-mates in their victory over Crystal Palace.

item.title: Title-winning experience 'massive' advantage for Celtic - Tierney
item.description: Kieran Tierney hopes the trophy winning experience of interim manager Martin O'Neill and some of his Celtic team-mates will give them the edge in what he admits is now a four-horse race for the Scottish title.

item.title: NI have 'something to build on' under Lindsay
item.description: Northern Ireland captain Jackie Burns says interim manager Kris Lindsay has given the players "something to build on" despite a 2-0 defeat in Switzerland.

item.title: Arteta doubles down after rivals' set-piece criticism
item.description: Mikel Arteta responds to criticism of Arsenal's tactics from rival managers by saying he is "upset" they "haven't scored more" from corners.

item.title: 'They look all over the shop' - Hart
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Leon Osman and Joe Hart look at how Tottenham Hotspur's disorganisation played a big part in their 2-1 defeat against Fulham.

item.title: Chelsea won't achieve anything with disciplinary issues - Rosenior
item.description: Chelsea manager Liam Rosenior says his side's "very good team performance" was let down by "not managing moments" as they received a ninth red card in all competitions this season in a 2-1 defeat at Arsenal.

item.title: Arteta delighted with win over 'top side' Chelsea
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta is delighted with the win against "top side" Chelsea which as his side came out 2-1 winners at the Emirates.

item.title: Starting Sesko is not a gamble - Carrick
item.description: Manchester United manager Michael Carrick says it is "not a gamble" to change his preferred XI to start striker Benjamin Sesko, after the Slovenian scored the winner against Crystal Palace for his sixth goal in eight games.

item.title: 'Handball rule is a mess' - Shearer unimpressed by Burnley VAR decision
item.description: 'Handball rule is a mess' - Alan Shearer unimpressed by Burnley VAR decision.

item.title: 'Out of absolutely nothing' - Lamine Yamal's solo stunner
item.description: Lamine Yamal goes on a brilliant solo run before curling an effort into the far corner to score his second goal, en route to his first career hat-trick in Barcelona's 4-1 win over Villarreal in La Liga.

item.title: Sutton's predictions v singer-songwriter & Chelsea fan Noah Kahan
item.description: BBC Sport football expert Chris Sutton takes on singer-songwriter Noah Kahan plus the BBC readers and AI with his predictions for this week's midweek Premier League fixtures on Tuesday, Wednesday and Thursday.

item.title: Messi, McTominay, Kane or Estevao - who to watch with 100 days to World Cup
item.description: With 100 days until the World Cup, BBC Sport looks at the favourites to win, as well as players to watch and who could be outside contenders.

item.title: Iran, the US, and a World Cup that starts in three months
item.description: What does the US conflict with Iran mean for what was already set to be a highly politicised World Cup this summer?

item.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
item.description: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

item.title: How do Lionesses solve full-back succession problem?
item.description: Will the Lionesses find a solution for their full-back problem in time for the 2027 Women's World Cup?

item.title: So much for three-year plan - what is the secret to manager longevity?
item.description: In his BBC Sport column, ex-Premier League boss Tony Pulis explains why it is so hard for football managers to keep their jobs in the modern game.

item.title: All February's done deals, including deadline day, in one place
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: Which club has the most red cards in a Premier League season?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the record for most red cards in a Premier League season

item.title: What is St Totteringham's Day and when could it happen?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains what St Totteringham's Day is and what its origins are

item.title: VAR vs TMO – what are the key differences?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything looks at the key differences between football's video assistant referees and rugby union's use of television match officials.

item.title: Who has the most Premier League appearances?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything Team look at which players have the highest total Premier League appearances.

item.title: Where do iconic football moves originate from?
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team explain where the names of iconic football moves come from.

item.title: Why is there a four-week break in the WSL?
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at why there is a second hiatus in the Women's Super League so soon after the winter break.

item.title: From 'scary' beginnings, McGlynn's Falkirk are 'miles ahead'
item.description: John McGlynn says Falkirk are "miles ahead" of the plan they made for a club he found "scary" when he became manager in 2022.

item.title: Celtic duo face setback in return from injury - gossip
item.description: Injury setback for Celtic duo, Rangers defensive poser and Hibs keep things ticking over during free weekend.

item.title: Chelsea to face holders PSG in Champions League last 16
item.description: Chelsea will play holders Paris St-Germain in the Champions League last 16 while Manchester City will face Real Madrid in the knockout stages for a fifth straight season.

item.title: From Hearts failure to Estoril excitement - the 'most Portuguese Scot there is'
item.description: After a forgettable seven months in charge at Hearts in 2017, Scottish manager Ian Cathro is rebuilding his reputation at Estoril.

item.title: Ronaldo becomes co-owner of Spanish side Almeria
item.description: Cristiano Ronaldo becomes part-owner of Spanish second division side Almeria after acquiring a 25% share in the Saudi Arabian-owned club.

item.title: PSG's Hakimi to face trial after rape allegation
item.description: Paris St-Germain defender Achraf Hakimi says he is set to face trial after an allegation of rape was made against him.

item.title: Barcelona sign Norwich prospect Tavares
item.description: Spanish giants Barcelona have signed 16-year-old Norwich City academy prospect Ajay Tavares, who has played for England age-group teams.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?

item.title: When River Plate icon Diaz took over fourth-tier Oxford
item.description: Twenty years on from Ramon Diaz's surprising appointment at Oxford United, BBC Sport looks at what happened and how it went.
